policy: Keep every Hermes capability with a plausible Roblox creator story, but expose it only through Playro product allowlists. Archive unrelated or risky generic Hermes surfaces and treat generated artifacts separately from source retention.

buckets.active: Required for the default Playro desktop/backend experience or repeatable verification path.
buckets.optional: Kept in repo but hidden, disabled, or gated by settings/project type until a Roblox creator workflow needs it.
buckets.archive_only: Should not be active on hermes/main; recover from the old Hermes archive branch if needed.
buckets.remove_generated: Generated or local build artifacts that are not source-retention decisions. Warn by default; do not delete automatically.

rules.reason: Primary Playro desktop app, backend, generator, tests, packaging, and product-local Hermes bridge.
Product-local Playro marketing and launch surface.
Repeatable Playro desktop/server verification path.
Product-local Playro packaging, smoke, validation, and release automation.
Roblox-specific validation or handoff support.
Hermes agent runtime can support Playro orchestration behind product boundaries.
Tool implementations may support Playro generation, validation, research, packaging, and automation when allowlisted.
Gateway adapters can enable future Discord, Telegram, webhook, API, or mobile build loops.
Background job runtime can power opt-in 24/7 Playro builder mode.
Provider routing can improve Playro quality modes and model selection.
CLI support layer remains useful for setup, tests, automation, and backend integration.
Plugin backends may support memory, provider, asset, orchestration, and future Roblox adapters when gated.
Skill retention is governed in detail by playro_skill_manifest.json; broad skills stay hidden or gated unless product-framed.
Optional skills may have Roblox creator stories but must be hidden or gated by Playro allowlists.
Root Hermes runtime entry point can support backend testing and automation behind Playro.
Model tooling can support Playro provider routing and quality improvements.
Toolset metadata can support product-local tool exposure policy.
Toolset distribution support may help future product packaging and allowlists.
Root Hermes support modules can back runtime, logging, state, and bootstrap behavior.
Root CLI remains a support layer for testing and automation, not default product UI.
Batch jobs may support evaluation, smoke generation, or bulk project validation.
Historical plans and docs remain useful for product context and audit traceability.
Blockchain skills lack a Playro Roblox creator story by default.
Finance modeling is outside Playro Roblox game creation scope.
Godmode/jailbreak red-team behavior must not be active in Playro.
Jailbreak/red-team inference behavior is out of product scope.
OSINT/account-hunting behavior is outside Playro creator workflows.
Unraid is only a build/test host, never Playro product behavior.
Homelab/server-management behavior is outside Playro product scope.
Generic server administration must not become Playro product behavior.
Python package metadata generated by build/install commands.
Python bytecode cache.
Python bytecode artifact.
Local pytest cache.
Generated local Playro project output unless intentionally promoted to a sample.
Desktop build output generated by packaging scripts.
Release staging output generated by desktop packaging.
